1: use std::collections::HashMap;
2: use ox_type_converter::{ValueType, TypeConverter, CONVERSION_REGISTRY};

6: /// Represents a single attribute with its value, type, and conversion parameters.
7: #[derive(Debug)]
8: pub struct AttributeValue {
9:     pub value: Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>,
10:     pub value_type: ValueType,
11:     pub value_type_parameters: HashMap<String, String>,
12: }
13: 
14: // Manual implementation of Clone for AttributeValue is tricky because of Box<dyn Any>.
15: // For rollback, we will move the value out and back in, avoiding the need for Clone.
16: 
17: impl AttributeValue {
18:     pub fn new<T: Any + Send + Sync + Clone + 'static>(value: T, value_type: ValueType) -> Self {
19:         AttributeValue {
20:             value: Box::new(value),
21:             value_type,
22:             value_type_parameters: HashMap::new(),
23:         }
24:     }
25: 
26:     pub fn with_parameters(mut self, parameters: HashMap<String, String>) -> Self {
27:         self.value_type_parameters = parameters;
28:         self
29:     }
30: 
31:     pub fn get_value<T: Clone + 'static>(&self) -> Option<T> {
32:         self.value.downcast_ref::<T>().cloned()
33:     }
34: 
35:     pub fn is<T: 'static>(&self) -> bool {
36:         self.value.is::<T>()
37:     }
38: 
39:     pub fn type_id(&self) -> TypeId {
40:         self.value.type_id()
41:     }
42: 
43:     pub fn to_string(&self) -> String {
44:         if let Some(s) = self.value.downcast_ref::<String>() {
45:             s.clone()
46:         } else if let Some(s) = self.value.downcast_ref::<&str>() {
47:             s.to_string()
48:         } else if let Some(i) = self.value.downcast_ref::<i32>() {
49:             i.to_string()
50:         } else if let Some(f) = self.value.downcast_ref::<f64>() {
51:             f.to_string()
52:         } else if let Some(b) = self.value.downcast_ref::<bool>() {
53:             b.to_string()
54:         } else {
55:             format!("{:?}", self.value)
56:         }
57:     }
58: }
59: 
60: /// The main generic data object structure
61: #[derive(Debug)]
62: pub struct GenericDataObject {
63:     attributes: HashMap<String, AttributeValue>,
64:     pub identifier_name: String,
65: }
66: 
67: impl GenericDataObject {
68:     /// Create a new GenericDataObject with a required unique identifier.
69:     pub fn new(identifier_name: &str, id: Option<Uuid>) -> Self {
70:         let mut object = Self {
71:             attributes: HashMap::new(),
72:             identifier_name: identifier_name.to_string(),
73:         };
74: 
75:         let guid = id.unwrap_or_else(Uuid::new_v4);
76:         // Store the UUID as a string for universal compatibility and serialization.
77:         // This initial set should not fail, so we can safely expect success.
78:         object.set(identifier_name, guid.to_string());
79: 
80:         object
81:     }
82: 
83:     /// Get a value from the attributes HashMap.
84:     /// This function calls BeforeGet and AfterGet callbacks.
85:     /// Get a value from the attributes HashMap.
86:     pub fn get<T: Clone + 'static + Default>(&self, identifier: &str) -> Option<T>
87:     where
88:         T: Any + Clone,
89:     {
90:         let attr_value = self.attributes.get(identifier)?;
91: 
92:         if let Some(value) = attr_value.get_value::<T>() {
93:             Some(value)
94:         } else {
95:             let string_value = attr_value.to_string();
96:             let target_type = TypeConverter::infer_value_type(&T::default());
97:             let registry = CONVERSION_REGISTRY.lock().unwrap();
98:             match registry.convert_with_specific_converter(
99:                 &attr_value.value_type.as_str(),
100:                 &target_type.as_str(),
101:                 &string_value,
102:                 &attr_value.value_type_parameters
103:             ) {
104:                 Ok(value) => value.downcast_ref::<T>().cloned(),
105:                 Err(_) => None,
106:             }
107:         }
108:     }
109: 
110:     /// Set a value in the attributes HashMap
111:     /// Set a value in the attributes HashMap.
112:     /// Returns the old `AttributeValue` if an attribute was replaced, or `None` otherwise.
113:     pub fn set<T: Any + Send + Sync + Clone + 'static>(&mut self, identifier: &str, value: T) -> Option<AttributeValue> {
114:         let value_type = TypeConverter::infer_value_type(&value);
115:         self.set_with_type(identifier, value, value_type, None)
116:     }
117: 
118:     /// Set a value with explicit type and parameters.
119:     /// Returns the old `AttributeValue` if an attribute was replaced, or `None` otherwise.
120:     pub fn set_with_type<T: Any + Send + Sync + Clone + 'static>(
121:         &mut self, 
122:         identifier: &str, 
123:         value: T, 
124:         value_type: ValueType,
125:         parameters: Option<HashMap<String, String>>
126:     ) -> Option<AttributeValue> {
127:         let mut new_attr_value = AttributeValue::new(value, value_type);
128:         if let Some(params) = parameters {
129:             new_attr_value = new_attr_value.with_parameters(params);
130:         }
131:         self.attributes.insert(identifier.to_string(), new_attr_value)
132:     }
133: 
134: 
135: 
136:     // --- Other methods ---
137: 
138:     pub fn get_attribute(&self, identifier: &str) -> Option<&AttributeValue> {
139:         self.attributes.get(identifier)
140:     }
